canonical_question: What are the best API versioning strategies?
aliases: URL path versioning vs header versioning
constraints: Never introduce breaking changes to a published API version without incrementing the version identifier
Maintain at most 2-3 active API versions simultaneously
Public APIs must use explicit versioning from day one
Sunset headers (RFC 8594) must accompany any deprecation
GraphQL APIs should use schema evolution, not URL versioning

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Building a GraphQL API and need schema evolution patterns
Need to design API rate limiting, not versioning
Building an internal microservice with a single consumer
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Consider contract testing (Pact) instead of formal versioning

often_confused_with.label: GraphQL Schema Patterns — uses schema evolution, not URL versioning
Semantic Versioning (SemVer) — library versioning, not API endpoint versioning

inputs_needed.question: What type of API are you building?
How often do you expect breaking changes?
Do you control all API consumers, or is this a public API?
